message:
feat(microsoft365,telegram): multi-auth-mode provisioning automation with self-check and doctor diagnostics

Implement provisioning automation for the Microsoft 365 and Telegram
connectors, supporting three authentication modes: direct access token,
OAuth2 client_credentials flow, and secretless credential_id for egress
proxy injection.

Microsoft 365 connector:
- Introduce M365Auth enum with AccessToken and CredentialId variants,
  replacing the hardcoded bearer token approach with per-request auth
  header injection via apply_auth()
- Add JWT permission parsing (scp + roles claims) with required-permission
  enforcement during configure
- Implement OAuth2 client_credentials token exchange for app-only auth
- Add endpoint URL security validation: HTTPS enforcement, host allowlist
  (graph.microsoft.com / login.microsoftonline.com), IP literal rejection,
  with test-mode escape hatch for localhost
- Add health_check() with /me fallback to /organization for both delegated
  and application-permission flows
- Implement handle_self_check() returning SelfCheckReport with auth_mode,
  permissions, and degraded status for credential_id mode
- Add 4 new tests covering permission validation, client_credentials
  exchange, and self-check behavior

Telegram connector:
- Add TelegramAuthConfig enum with BotToken and CredentialId variants
- Implement strict bot token syntax validation enforcing <bot_id>:<secret>
  format (6+ digit bot_id, 20+ char alphanumeric secret)
- Add comprehensive handle_doctor() with 7 named diagnostic checks:
  configuration, auth_mode, poll_timeout bounds, allowed_updates
  validation, network constraints, token syntax, and token validation
- Enrich configure with runtime settings validation (poll_timeout range,
  allowed_updates dedup and known-type enforcement, base_url normalization)
- Report auth_mode in health responses; handle degraded state gracefully
  when configured with credential_id but no materialized token
- Add 4 new tests for syntax validation, ambiguous auth rejection, invalid
  token rejection, and doctor credential_id mode

Both connectors route new message handlers (self_check, doctor) in main.rs.
Update 5 beads tracking issues to in_progress with assignees.
Add .claude/settings.local.json to .gitignore.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -81,6 +81,7 @@ async fn handle_message(connector: &mut M365Connector, message: &str) -> serde_j
         "configure" => connector.handle_configure(params).await,
         "handshake" => connector.handle_handshake(params).await,
         "health" => connector.handle_health().await,
+        "self_check" => connector.handle_self_check().await,
         "introspect" => connector.handle_introspect().await,
         "invoke" => connector.handle_invoke(params).await,
         "simulate" => connector.handle_simulate(params).await,

@@ -113,6 +113,7 @@ async fn handle_message(connector: &mut TelegramConnector, message: &str) -> ser
         "configure" => connector.handle_configure(params).await,
         "handshake" => connector.handle_handshake(params).await,
         "health" => connector.handle_health().await,
+        "doctor" => connector.handle_doctor().await,
         "self_check" => connector.handle_self_check().await,
         "introspect" => connector.handle_introspect().await,
         "invoke" => connector.handle_invoke(params).await,
